title: Restart resume orchestrator integrating with KERNEL-001 SessionBroker
goal: Implement the restart resume orchestrator that, on Handshake process startup, discovers all sessions left in non-terminal states from the previous run, computes a ReplayPlan per session via MT-192, and hands reconstructed state back to the KERNEL-001 SessionBroker for resumption. This is the entrypoint that makes the cluster X crash recovery slice actually heal a crashed process.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/restart_resume.rs with `RestartResumeOrchestrator`, `ResumeReport`; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/restart_resume_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-191 (checkpoint writer) and MT-192 (replay) merged. MT-186 cancellation primitive merged so the orchestrator can surface resume-impossible sessions for cancellation rather than silent hang.
implementation_notes: Orchestrator runs once at startup, after database migrations, before SessionBroker accepts new traffic. Algorithm: (1) query KERNEL-001 session table for sessions where state IN ('Running', 'AwaitingVerification', 'Paused', 'CancellationRequested'); (2) for each session: (a) call StateReplayer::plan(session_id) (MT-192); (b) if plan succeeds, call StateReplayer::execute with the session's state_apply function (registered via a `ResumableSession` trait that KERNEL-001 SessionBroker implements); (c) if execute Ok, hand state to SessionBroker::resume(session_id, state) and mark resumed; (d) if execute Err (MissingEvent/StateInvariantViolated), mark session state='RecoveryFailed' and queue a decision_request mailbox message to operator role asking for cancel or manual rerun decision; (e) if ProcessOwnershipLedger shows orphan processes for this session, call reclaim hooks (MT-186 cancellation forced path) before resuming. Emits ResumeReport { sessions_examined, sessions_resumed: Vec<Uuid>, sessions_recovery_failed: Vec<(Uuid, ResumeError)>, total_replay_events, total_duration_ms } and writes ResumeReport to a `kernel_restart_resume_report` table for forensic audit. FR-EVT-RESTART-RESUME-{STARTED,SESSION_RESUMED,SESSION_RECOVERY_FAILED,COMPLETED} emitted per phase. Idempotent: if orchestrator runs twice on same database state, second run resumes 0 sessions (since first run transitioned them out of resumable states). Honors spec §5 line 1208: replay works with EventLedger alone, no Flight Recorder dependency. Tests: (a) seed db with 3 mid-run sessions + checkpoints + events, restart, expect 3 resumed; (b) seed with gap in event seq, expect 1 sessions_recovery_failed + decision_request mailbox message posted; (c) seed with orphan process, expect reclaim hook fired before resume; (d) idempotency: run twice, second time examines 0.
risk_if_missed: Without the restart orchestrator, MT-190/191/192 primitives are unwired libraries — a crashed Handshake never actually resumes; cluster X's central recovery promise has zero behavior.
validator_focus: Idempotency proven by second-run test (0 sessions examined); RecoveryFailed sessions always trigger operator decision_request (never silent hang); reclaim hooks invoked before resume (no double-process).

implementation_record.summary: MT-193 now includes both the folded restart-resume primitive and the production Postgres startup runner. main.rs initializes control-plane Postgres storage, runs migrations, invokes PostgresRestartResumeRunner before listener binding, resumes eligible kernel_session_queue rows from kernel_session_checkpoint plus kernel_event_ledger, persists kernel_restart_resume_report rows, emits typed restart-resume FR evidence, reclaims orphan kernel_process_lifecycle rows, posts real role_mailbox_v1 operator decision requests on recovery failure, and records target-specific idempotency keys for concrete Postgres writes.
implementation_record.contract_deviation_note: PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT is narrowed to the folded primitive: src/backend/handshake_core/src/session_checkpoint/restart.rs still owns the original in-memory primitive and is re-exported from process_ledger/restart_resume.rs. The production startup/Postgres/mailbox/report adapter is now wired in process_ledger/restart_resume.rs and src/backend/handshake_core/src/main.rs; the prior unwired-startup, missing-Postgres-adapter, missing-real-mailbox, and missing-real-report risks are closed by live Postgres tests.

red_team_coverage.idempotency: restart_resume_postgres_tests::mt193_postgres_startup_runner_resumes_persists_report_and_is_idempotent asserts first run resumes one session, persists report rows, writes target-specific idempotency keys for kernel_process_lifecycle/kernel_session_queue/kernel_session_checkpoint, and second run examines/resumes zero.
red_team_coverage.global_event_sequence: restart_resume_postgres_tests::mt193_postgres_startup_runner_replays_session_events_when_global_event_sequence_interleaves_other_sessions proves global ledger sequences occupied by other sessions are not treated as gaps in the replaying session.
red_team_coverage.operator_decision_request: restart_resume_postgres_tests::mt193_postgres_startup_runner_marks_recovery_failed_and_posts_operator_decision proves replay gaps mark the queue failed and insert real role_mailbox_thread/role_mailbox_message decision-request rows linked to the session.
red_team_coverage.reclaim_before_resume: The live Postgres success path reclaims orphan kernel_process_lifecycle rows before queue resume and records a target-specific kernel_process_lifecycle idempotency key.
red_team_coverage.report_persistence_and_fr: Live Postgres tests assert kernel_restart_resume_report rows with schema_version=2, orphan_reclaims/operator_decision_requests/fr_events_emitted evidence, and typed restart-resume FR event IDs.
red_team_coverage.startup_ordering: cargo check --features app-runtime --bin handshake_core proves main.rs compiles with control-plane storage initialization, migrations, restart-resume runner execution, then listener/AppState setup.

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: Closing MT-193 required touching startup/storage/migration surfaces beyond the original primitive-owned files because the contract requires process-startup behavior before SessionBroker traffic, real Postgres report persistence, real mailbox decision requests, and target-specific idempotency rows. Those surfaces are now wired and verified rather than parked as follow-on debt.

next_dependent_mts_unblocked: MT-194 target-specific idempotency is now consumed by MT-193 production restart-resume call sites for queue, checkpoint, mailbox, and process-lifecycle writes.
MT-195 can validate against live Postgres restart-resume report rows, mailbox decision rows, orphan reclaim evidence, and runtime chaos scenarios rather than only the folded in-memory primitive.
